notion vs trello for small business productivity — Complete Guide [2026]

AI AI Tools Idea · May 24, 2026 · Updated May 24, 2026
— min read
Affiliate disclosure: This article contains affiliate links. We may earn a commission if you sign up through our links — at no extra cost to you. Learn more →
🚀 AI Platform

As a small business owner, you’re likely struggling to find the perfect tool to manage your team’s tasks, projects, and workflows, which is why you’re considering Notion vs Trello for small business productivity. With so many options available, it can be overwhelming to decide which one is best for your business. In this article, we’ll break down the features, pricing, and benefits of Notion and Trello to help you make an informed decision.

Notion and Trello are two of the most popular project management tools on the market, and for good reason. They both offer a range of features that can help small businesses streamline their workflows, improve collaboration, and increase productivity. However, they have some key differences that may make one more suitable for your business than the other.

Pro tip: Before choosing a project management tool, take some time to identify your business’s specific needs and pain points. What are your biggest challenges when it comes to managing tasks and projects? What features are must-haves for your team? By understanding your needs, you can make a more informed decision and choose a tool that will truly help your business thrive.

Notion is an all-in-one workspace that allows teams to manage tasks, projects, and workflows in a single platform. It offers a range of features, including notes, tasks, databases, and pages, which can be customized to fit your business’s specific needs. Notion also integrates with a range of third-party apps, including Google Drive, Slack, and Trello, making it easy to incorporate into your existing workflow.

Trello, on the other hand, is a visual project management tool that uses boards, lists, and cards to organize tasks and projects. It’s known for its Kanban-style workflow, which allows teams to move cards across different lists as they progress through a project. Trello also offers a range of features, including due dates, assignments, and checklists, which can help teams stay organized and on track.

Pro tip: If you’re considering using Trello, take some time to explore its power-up features, which can add additional functionality to your boards. For example, you can use the Calendar power-up to view your tasks and deadlines in a calendar format, or the Voting power-up to allow team members to vote on tasks and priorities.

In terms of pricing, Notion and Trello both offer free plans, as well as paid upgrades with additional features. As of 2026, Notion’s pricing plans are as follows:

  • Free: $0/month (unlimited pages and blocks, 100MB storage)
  • Personal: $4/month (1GB storage, unlimited pages and blocks)
  • Team: $8/month (1GB storage, unlimited pages and blocks, team features)
  • Enterprise: custom pricing for large teams and organizations

Trello’s pricing plans are as follows:

  • Free: $0/month (unlimited boards, lists, and cards, 250MB storage)
  • Standard: $5/month (250MB storage, unlimited boards, lists, and cards)
  • Premium: $10/month (1GB storage, unlimited boards, lists, and cards, advanced features)
  • Enterprise: custom pricing for large teams and organizations

Here’s a comparison table to help you visualize the features and pricing of Notion and Trello:

FeatureNotionTrello
Price$0-$8/month$0-$10/month
FeaturesNotes, tasks, databases, pagesBoards, lists, cards, due dates, assignments
Ease of use8/109/10
Best forTeams that need an all-in-one workspaceTeams that need a visual project management tool
Support24/7 support, online resources24/7 support, online resources

Pro tip: If you’re considering using Notion, take some time to explore its template gallery, which offers a range of pre-built templates for common use cases, such as project management, customer relationship management, and marketing campaigns. These templates can help you get started quickly and customize your workspace to fit your business’s specific needs.

Based on our comparison, we recommend Notion for teams that need an all-in-one workspace with a range of features and customization options. We recommend Trello for teams that need a visual project management tool with a simple, intuitive interface.

If you’re looking for a tool that can help you manage complex workflows and projects, Notion may be the better choice. Its database feature allows you to create custom tables and relations, which can help you track and manage complex data. Additionally, its page feature allows you to create custom pages and templates, which can help you create a tailored workspace for your team.

On the other hand, if you’re looking for a tool that can help you manage simple tasks and projects, Trello may be the better choice. Its Kanban-style workflow is easy to use and understand, and its boards and lists can be customized to fit your team’s specific needs.

In terms of support, both Notion and Trello offer 24/7 support and online resources, including documentation, tutorials, and community forums. However, Notion’s support team is known for its responsiveness and helpfulness, which can be a major advantage for teams that need help getting started or troubleshooting issues.

Overall, the choice between Notion and Trello will depend on your business’s specific needs and preferences. We recommend trying out both tools to see which one works best for your team.

To get started with Notion, Try Notion free today and see how it can help your business thrive.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the main difference between Notion and Trello?

Notion is an all-in-one workspace that allows teams to manage tasks, projects, and workflows in a single platform, while Trello is a visual project management tool that uses boards, lists, and cards to organize tasks and projects. Notion offers a range of features, including notes, tasks, databases, and pages, which can be customized to fit your business’s specific needs.

Can I use Notion and Trello together?

Yes, you can use Notion and Trello together. Notion integrates with Trello, allowing you to connect your Trello boards and cards to your Notion pages and databases. This can help you create a seamless workflow between the two tools and get the most out of their features.

How do I choose between Notion and Trello for my small business?

To choose between Notion and Trello, consider your business’s specific needs and pain points. What are your biggest challenges when it comes to managing tasks and projects? What features are must-haves for your team? By understanding your needs, you can make a more informed decision and choose a tool that will truly help your business thrive.

What are some common use cases for Notion and Trello?

Notion is commonly used for project management, customer relationship management, and marketing campaigns, while Trello is commonly used for task management, agile development, and team collaboration. Both tools can be customized to fit a range of use cases, so it’s worth exploring their features and templates to see what works best for your business.
